scenario: Pinnacle Logistics tracks 9000 shipments per day in Camunda 8.8 and a developer must compute the number of days between a planned pickup date and the carrier confirmation date using a FEEL subtraction expression inside a DMN decision input column on the routing decision.
question: Which result type does the FEEL subtraction operator return when both operands are date values?

options.text: A date value equal to the earlier of the two operands, with the later operand silently dropped from the calculation by the operator
A years-months-duration value rounded down to whole months between the two date operands at evaluation time by the FEEL engine
A days-time-duration value capturing the elapsed days between the operands, as in date("2020-04-06") - date("2020-04-01") = duration("P5D")
A date-time value at midnight on the later date, with the timezone copied from the system default of the Zeebe broker at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL never silently drops an operand; subtracting two dates returns a duration result that represents the gap, not one of the input dates.

b.text: Incorrect. Two date operands produce a days-time-duration, not a years-months-duration; the years-months result only arises when subtracting two years-months-duration values.

c.text: Correct. The Subtraction table shows that date - date returns a days-time-duration, illustrated by date("2020-04-06") - date("2020-04-01") evaluating to duration("P5D").

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL subtraction of two dates returns a duration, not a date-time; timezone defaults and broker settings play no role in this operator's result type.

explanation: The FEEL Subtraction operator maps date - date to a days-time-duration. The docs show date("2020-04-06") - date("2020-04-01") evaluating to duration("P5D"), giving the elapsed-days gap directly without any timezone or rounding intervention.
